text:
Market today: Mixed-to-cautious tone. Quant funds just had their worst momentum rout of the year - that's directly relevant to you because momentum IS your book (tech, chips, leveraged ETFs). When quant unwinds hit, high-flying names get sold indiscriminately. Inflation staying 'significantly above target' in Europe adds pressure on rate-sensitive growth stocks. Not a panic day, but not a tailwind either.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the loudest alarm - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF, decay is real, and on margin a 7% hole gets ugly fast; stop is $200, price is $236, so you have room but watch it hard. SMH (-0.69%) is softer than it should be given SOXL is a 3x version of essentially the same bet - confirms chip sector weakness. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; on margin any gap-down hits hard, size it tiny.

Watch: $200 stop on SOXL - if chips roll over on this quant unwind, that level gets tested. Also watch if the momentum rout deepens; CRWD at $742 could stall fast.

Bottom line: Protect the leveraged positions first - today is a day to tighten, not add.
plays: SOXL: stop is $200 - consider moving it up closer to $215-220 given you're already down 7% on margin. No reason to let a 3x leveraged position run further against you.
CRWD: scanner shows accumulate entry $741-742, stop $740.31 - but that's a razor-thin stop on a name already in premium. Hold what you have; skip adding unless it pulls back cleanly.
No new buys today - quant momentum unwind is active, your leveraged and chip names are already under pressure; adding here is fighting the tape.
